notes:
# v0.5.0 — Mermaid rendering + Phase 2.0 polish

Feature + polish release. Adds Mermaid diagram rendering to the Markdown reader, and closes two debt items from Phase 2.0.5.

## New

- **Mermaid block rendering** — Fenced ` ```mermaid ` code blocks in any `.md` file are now rendered as visual diagrams instead of raw source. Light/dark theme follows the app theme. Malformed diagrams render as an inline error placeholder instead of crashing the preview. Mermaid (~600 KB minified) is **lazy-loaded** on the first overlay open so the cold-start path is unaffected.

## Polish

- **Global hotkey reliability after auto-update** — Pressing `Ctrl+Alt+A` immediately after an auto-update install used to occasionally trigger a spurious "another app owns it" warning. Root cause: the updater's `taskkill /F` of the previous version doesn't run the `unregister(HOTKEY)` cleanup, leaving a brief OS-level binding to the dead process. `useGlobalShortcut.ts` now retries `register()` once after 1.5 seconds, covering the OS reap window. If both attempts fail, the warning toast is correctly shown for real conflicts.
- **Debug logs stripped** — The `[gs]` instrumentation added in v0.4.3 has been removed. The DevTools feature flag (`tauri = { features = ["devtools"] }`) is **kept enabled** — F12 / `--auto-open-devtools-for-tabs` still opens the inspector for future debugging.

## Architecture notes

- **Markdown fence renderer**: `useMarkdown.ts` now short-circuits `lang === "mermaid"` to emit a `<div class="mermaid-placeholder" data-source="...">` instead of going through Shiki. `MarkdownReader.vue`'s post-render hook picks these up via `querySelectorAll`, lazy-imports `mermaid`, calls `mermaid.render()`, and swaps the placeholder for the resulting `<svg>` inside a `.mermaid-container` wrapper. Theme switch resets the init flag and re-renders.
- **Hotkey retry pattern**: The current implementation makes 2 attempts (initial + 1 retry after 1500ms). If a real third-party app holds `Ctrl+Alt+A` (PowerToys, OBS, etc.) the warning toast surfaces 1.5 s later than before — negligible UX delta given the better behaviour on the (much more common) auto-update path.

## Tests

- New `tests/e2e/phase-2-1-5-mermaid.mjs` — opens `phase-1-mvp/recording-script.md` (7 known mermaid blocks), verifies the DOM contains 7 `.mermaid-container` nodes + 0 `.mermaid-error` nodes + 0 leftover `.mermaid-placeholder` nodes.
- `tests/e2e/phase-2-0-hotkey.mjs` updated — switched from `[gs]` log assertions to `isMinimized` state polling (the logs were intentionally removed in this release).
- Phase 1 e2e clean (9/0/1). Phase 2.1 + 2.7 each 1/4 fail = pre-existing HD2 + HD5 documented in `phase-2-0/validation-log.md`.

## Known limitations

- Mermaid diagrams are static SVG — no zoom, pan, click handlers, or export. Phase 2.5+ candidate if dogfooding requests.
- KaTeX (math) + Frontmatter rendering still **out of scope** — dogfood-notes confirmed 0 hits in 28-file scan.
- Mermaid bundle ~600 KB minified. First overlay open with a mermaid block has a one-time download cost; subsequent renders are instant.
- v0.4.x installs cannot auto-update to v0.5.0 because the Phase 2.0 endpoint trust chain was rebuilt in v0.4.1 — manual install via MSI only for v0.5.0 first-time. From v0.5.0 onward auto-update works as designed.

## Installers

- `ai-dev-tool_0.5.0_x64_en-US.msi` — minisign-signed
- `ai-dev-tool_0.5.0_x64_en-US.msi.sig`
- `ai-dev-tool_0.5.0_x64-setup.exe`

## What's next

- Continue dogfooding v0.5.0 — accumulate Phase 2.5 (pain points) candidates
- Phase 2.0.5 remaining items (RP2 env-var, HD2 launch-debuggable, HD5 phase-2-7 layout) — close opportunistically when next touching their respective areas
- Roadmap: Phase 2.2 (Explorer power) is the next significant phase candidate
